THE_THREE_GRAPHS.note: *** Three DIFFERENT graphs, three DIFFERENT meanings. Conflating them was the semantic defect the v1 index shipped. Every SCC / cycle / layering claim below states WHICH graph it is about. ***

G1_compile_time_import_graph.meaning: *** A HARD LOAD-ORDER CONSTRAINT. Resolved at import time. A cycle here is load-order sensitive and can become an ImportError at process start. ***
G1_compile_time_import_graph.is_it_a_DAG: *** NO — it contains one non-trivial SCC. Its SCC-CONDENSATION is a DAG (G1c). ***

G1c_SCC_condensed_compile_graph.definition: G1 with every strongly-connected component collapsed to a single node
G1c_SCC_condensed_compile_graph.meaning: *** A DAG BY CONSTRUCTION (a condensation always is). This is the graph 'layer level' is defined on, and the ONLY sense in which FanOps has a layered compile-time structure. ***

G2_static_potential_dependency_graph.meaning: *** A STATIC OVER-APPROXIMATION OF RUNTIME DEPENDENCY. A lazy import materializes ONLY IF its enclosing function is called. An SCC here means 'these modules COULD reach each other', NOT 'they DO'. It BOUNDS the blast radius; it does not ESTABLISH it. ***

G3_runtime_call_graph.status: *** NOT DERIVED by any cycle. G2 is a strict SUPERSET of it. No claim in this KB rests on G3. ***
G3_runtime_call_graph.why_it_matters: providers.py dispatches six backend factories via lazy in-function import LAMBDAS from a dict. A NAME-BASED call graph flags all six as 'zero callers'. ALL SIX ARE LIVE (COUP-10).

provenance.method: AST pass over all 127 modules. Imports classified by SYNTACTIC POSITION (module-level / in-function / TYPE_CHECKING / try-except ImportError).
provenance.cross_validation: Independently reproduced Cycle-3's censuses EXACTLY: subprocess=32, Ledger.transaction=74, mkdtemp=1, rmtree=2.
provenance.level_definition: *** CORRECTED. *** level(SCC) = longest-path depth over the SCC-CONDENSATION of G1 (a DAG by construction); every module inherits its SCC's level. The v1 index used a cycle-cutting DFS (`if m in seen: return 0`) which is ENTRY-ORDER-DEPENDENT for nodes inside an SCC. That produced 6 wrong module levels and an upward-edge count of 106. The corrected count is 107.

CYCLE_7_RECONCILIATION.status: *** THESE NUMBERS ARE DERIVED_FROM_CODE. THEY ROT. *** They were correct at fcffa73 and WRONG one commit later (#634) — and then rotted AGAIN, while Cycle 7's own PR was open (#635). Twice in two commits. The authoritative twin is derived/dependencies.json; rule ARCH-009 now fails CI when they disagree. MIGRATION TARGET: delete this block and point at the derived twin.
CYCLE_7_RECONCILIATION.updated_at_HEAD: modules 127->129->130 · compile_edges_G1 528->539->548->549 · runtime_lazy_edges 323->324->327. #634 added framing_outcomes + reframe; #635 added reframe_apply; #640 added reframe_apply->controlio (sidecar write delegated to write_json_atomic).
CYCLE_7_RECONCILIATION.what_did_NOT_move: *** The layering still did not get worse. lateral stays 51, G1c_levels stays 11, and there is still exactly ONE non-trivial compile-time SCC (the persona triple). strictly_upward went 56->57 and the G2 SCC grew 45->47 — #635's new module joined the existing lazy-cycle mass rather than creating a new inversion. No new cycle, no new level. ***
CYCLE_7_RECONCILIATION.G2_module_sccs_is_AMBIGUOUS: The value 8 is the NON-TRIVIAL SCC count. The TOTAL (including singletons) is 69. Cycle 5 never stated which it meant; its enumerated `all_sccs` list makes it the non-trivial one. derived/dependencies.json now emits BOTH, named (`G2_non_trivial_sccs`, `G2_module_sccs_total`).
CYCLE_7_RECONCILIATION.independent_revalidation: Cycle 7 rebuilt this graph with a fresh extractor and reproduced Cycle 5 EXACTLY at fcffa73 on 17 of 18 censuses — including all of 528 / 323 / 6 / 107 / 56 / 51 / 45 / 11 / 1. Cycle 5's graph is CONFIRMED, not merely inherited.

G1_the_ONE_compile_time_module_cycle.status: *** THE ONLY non-trivial SCC in the compile-time graph. 124 of 127 modules are SINGLETON SCCs. ***
G1_the_ONE_compile_time_module_cycle.load_order_sensitivity: Python resolves it via partial-module binding. It works today. A reordering of imports inside personas.py could turn it into an ImportError at process start, and nothing would catch it before deploy.
G1_the_ONE_compile_time_module_cycle.is_it_related_to_the_learning_loop: *** NO. It is in the persona/registry subsystem and has NOTHING to do with the select->learn feedback loop. ***
G1_the_ONE_compile_time_module_cycle.intentionality: UNKNOWN (UNK-C5-1). Undefended: no comment, no test, no ADR.

THE_DOMAIN_CORE_IS_ACYCLIC_AT_MODULE_LEV.statement: *** The modules implementing the learning loop — moments, adjust, caption, clip, moment_hook_learning, track, accounts — are ALL SINGLETON SCCs in G1. THERE IS NO MODULE-LEVEL IMPORT CYCLE AMONG THEM. ***
THE_DOMAIN_CORE_IS_ACYCLIC_AT_MODULE_LEV.why_it_matters: The feedback loop is REALIZED WITHOUT a module-level import cycle. That is arguably GOOD design — and the v1 index OBSCURED it by asserting 'the domain core is a compile-time cycle', a statement true ONLY of the AGGREGATED subsystem view.
THE_DOMAIN_CORE_IS_ACYCLIC_AT_MODULE_LEV.confidence: CERTAIN (Tarjan over G1: 125 SCCs, 1 non-trivial, and it is the persona triple)

subsystem_level_cycle.WHAT_IT_DOES_MEAN: *** REAL COUPLING. These seven subsystems are mutually dependent: you cannot extract S08 as a library without S14. Every closing edge was traced to a real module-level edge and verified. ***
subsystem_level_cycle.WHAT_IT_DOES_NOT_MEAN: *** IT IS NOT AN IMPORT CYCLE. It is produced by GROUPING distinct modules into subsystem nodes: `moments -> moment_hook_learning` and `adjust -> moments` are two DIFFERENT module edges that collapse into a 2-cycle only once their modules are aggregated. It carries NONE of the load-order fragility of a real import cycle. ***
subsystem_level_cycle.boundary_correction_applied: An initial partition produced a 13-subsystem cycle. THREE closing edges proved to be MIS-DRAWN BOUNDARIES and were corrected. The 7-subsystem cycle is what SURVIVES.

intentionality_SPLIT.note: *** The v1 index CONFLATED two claims. They have DIFFERENT truth values and DIFFERENT confidence. ***

A_the_domain_feedback_loop.claim: FanOps IS a closed learning loop: select -> render -> caption -> publish -> measure -> learn -> select.
A_the_domain_feedback_loop.evidence: It is the product. The project's own CLAUDE.md documents the insights-culmination reach loop, the amplify/bias actuators and the validation gates. Not inference.

B_how_the_loop_is_WIRED.claim: The loop is realized by direct cross-module imports (adjust -> moments), producing a SUBSYSTEM-level cycle but NO module-level import cycle.
B_how_the_loop_is_WIRED.why_it_does_NOT_follow_from_A: *** A feedback loop in DATA/CONTROL FLOW does not require a cycle in the STATIC IMPORT GRAPH. The loop could equally be closed by the ORCHESTRATOR (pipeline calls both adjust and moments) or via a SHARED ABSTRACTION, leaving the subsystem graph acyclic. Dependency inversion exists precisely for this. The wiring is a DESIGN CHOICE, not a consequence of the loop existing. ***
B_how_the_loop_is_WIRED.the_v1_overclaim: *** The v1 index said of the subsystem cycle: 'This is not a defect ... the import graph is telling the truth about the product.' THAT WENT BEYOND THE EVIDENCE. The evidence supports 'the loop is real and expected.' It does NOT support 'this wiring is correct.' RETRACTED — C5-COR-09. ***
B_how_the_loop_is_WIRED.what_a_future_engineer_should_take_from_: The loop is the product and is not up for debate. The mutual-import WIRING is one way to build it; inverting it (orchestrator-closed, or via a shared interface) is a LEGITIMATE OPTION. A design decision to be MADE, not a given to be preserved.

layering.verdict: *** An 11-level DAG — BY CONSTRUCTION, because a condensation always is. It is NOT an ENFORCED layering: no test, no lint rule, no layer declaration exists. The level is an OUTPUT of the import structure, not a CONSTRAINT on it. ***

strictly_upward.meaning: *** TRUE LAYERING INVERSIONS. A module at level N lazily imports one at level > N. Legal ONLY because the import is deferred to call time. ***

lateral_same_level.meaning: Same-level deferred imports. NOT layering inversions. Recorded SEPARATELY because the v1 index lumped them in with the inversions and reported one number.

layering.the_central_fact: *** `config` — level 0, fan-in 82, THE most-depended-on module in the system — lazily reaches UP to `accounts` (level 2) and `meta_graph` (level 3). The base of the stack calls the middle of it. ***
layering.the_risk: Module initialization order is LOAD-BEARING and NOTHING ENFORCES IT. Hoisting any one of the 56 strictly-upward lazy imports to module level — a change that LOOKS LIKE A CLEANUP — breaks the process at start.
layering.supersedes: COUP-09 (Cycle 2) recorded NINE such imports and framed them as a deliberate, bounded device. The nine are a SUBSET of 107.

G2_static_potential_dependency_sccs.QUALIFIER: *** POTENTIAL-DEPENDENCY SCCs, NOT DEMONSTRATED RUNTIME COUPLING. A lazy edge materializes only if its enclosing function is called. G2 is a SUPERSET of the runtime call graph. These SCCs BOUND the blast radius; they do NOT establish it. ***

largest.note: includes config, ledger, accounts, clip, moments, post.*, personas, transcribe, framing, caption

subsystem_scc.meaning: *** In the STATIC POTENTIAL-DEPENDENCY graph every subsystem can POTENTIALLY reach every other. An UPPER BOUND on coupling, not a measurement of it. ***

optional_fail_CLOSED: cv2 (opencv) — the ONLY fail-closed optional dep; framing.require_cv2 raises ToolchainMissingError -> exit 2 when smart_framing is ON (default). INV-17.
external_dependencies.evidence: AST: try/except ImportError sites = config->certifi, framing->cv2, studio.app->flask, transcribe->faster_whisper|whisper

graph.note: THE GRAPH ITSELF. compile_edges = module-level imports (a hard load-order dependency). runtime_lazy_edges = in-function imports (deferred to call time). typing_only_edges = under `if TYPE_CHECKING:` (no runtime dependency at all).
